post.post_content: Comprehensive, A to Z list of all dog breeds with pictures. Search and sort by size, country of origin, alphabet and more. Use the arrow at the right of the search box to select a search area. For example; choose "alpha" and enter a letter to sort the list alphabetically or select "details" and search a country for a list of all breeds with a certain origin, size or shedding.
post.post_title: Complete List of All Dog Breeds

value.description: The Affenpinscher is a small sized dog breed originating from France and Germany. Its history can be traced back to the 17th century, where it was used as a rat catcher in homes and stables. Despite its original purpose, the Affenpinscher is an energetic, playful, and affectionate breed with a strong sense of confidence. Its coat is thick and shaggy, and comes in standard colors in shades of yellow, red, grey and pure black. Although the Affenpinscher has been a popular breed in Europe for many years, it is still considered to be a relatively rare breed outside of its native countries. Throughout its history, the Affenpinscher has been valued for its ability to catch vermin and its charming personality. Today, it remains a beloved breed for many dog owners around the world as a companion and show dog

value.description:
Read more facts on our dedicated Afghan Hound page


The Afghan Hound is a medium sized breed originating from Afghanistan. Its history can be traced back to ancient times, where it was used as a hunting and companion dog by nomadic tribes. Despite its hunting history, the Afghan Hound is an independent, aloof, and graceful breed with a regal demeanor. Its coat is silky, fine, and long, and, according to FCI breed standard, comes in all colors but should not have white markings on the head or collar. Although the Afghan Hound has been a cherished breed in its native country for many centuries, it has gained popularity around the world as a show dog and companion. Throughout its history, the Afghan Hound has been valued for its speed, elegance, and beauty. Today, it remains a beloved breed for many dog owners around the world who appreciate its unique appearance and dignified demeanor.

value.description: The Aidi, also known as the Atlas Mountain Dog or the "Chien de l'Atlas," is a medium-sized, muscular breed of dog that originates from the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. They have a thick, dense coat that ranges in color from white to shades of fawn, brown and/or black, and they have a distinctive, shaggy mane around their neck. They are an independent and strong-willed breed, known for their loyalty and protective nature. They were historically used as guard dogs, and they are still used today as working dogs in rural areas. They are relatively rare outside of their native region.

value.description:
Read more Airedale Terrier facts on our dedicated Airedale Terrier page.


The Airedale Terrier is a medium sized dog breed originating from the United Kingdom. Its history can be traced back to the 19th century, where it was developed by crossing the Old English Terrier with the Otterhound. The breed was originally used for hunting otters, as well as for herding and guarding livestock. Despite its working history, the Airedale Terrier is an energetic, fearless, and affectionate breed with a strong sense of loyalty. Its coat is wiry and dense, and comes in standard colors of saddle black, grizzle and tan. Although the Airedale Terrier has been a popular breed in its native country for many years, it is still considered to be a relatively rare breed outside of the United Kingdom. Throughout its history, the Airedale Terrier has been valued for its courage, intelligence, and versatility. Today, it remains a beloved breed for many dog owners around the world as a companion, working dog, and show dog.

value.description:
Our Akita description and more information can be found on our Akita Facts post.

The Japanese Akita Inu is a large, powerful dog breed originating from Japan, specifically the Akita region. It has a long history, dating back hundreds of years, where it was used for hunting large game like bears and boars, as well as for guarding and companionship. Akitas are known for their loyal, courageous, and independent temperament. They can be reserved with strangers but are deeply devoted to their families, making them good family pets for experienced owners. However, they may not be ideal for first-time dog owners due to their strong-willed nature. The breed is not extremely rare but is less common outside Japan. Interestingly, the Akita Inu is considered a national treasure in Japan and is often seen as a symbol of good health, happiness, and longevity. Hachiko, the famous loyal Akita, is a well-known symbol of the breed's devotion.

value.description:
For a description and more in depth information, visit our post dedicated to facts about the Alaskan Malamute.

The Alaskan Malamute is one of the oldest Arctic sled dog breeds, developed by the Mahlemut Inuit tribe in Alaska thousands of years ago. Traditionally, they were used for hauling heavy freight over long distances in harsh conditions. Malamutes are known for their strong, friendly, and loyal temperament, but they can be independent and stubborn. They make good family pets for active households but require experienced owners due to their strength and energy. The breed is not rare but is less common than some other working dogs. Malamutes have a thick double coat, and standard colors include shades of gray, black, sable, and red, often with white markings. Interestingly, they are not built for speed but for endurance and strength, making them excellent for pulling heavy loads. They are also known for their expressive, wolf-like appearance and love of howling.

value.description:
You can find in depth facts and details about the Akita in our dedicated Akita post.

The American Akita, originally derived from the Japanese Akita Inu, was developed in the United States after World War II. It was bred to be larger and more robust than its Japanese counterpart. Traditionally, the breed was used for guarding, hunting, and companionship. American Akitas are known for their strong, confident, and loyal temperament, but they can be aloof with strangers and dominant with other dogs. They can make good family pets for experienced owners who can handle their strong personality. The breed is not extremely rare but is less common than many other breeds. Unlike the Japanese Akita, the American Akita has a broader head, heavier build, and comes in a wider variety of colors, including black masks and pinto patterns. The Japanese Akita is more fox-like, with a narrower build and limited color options.

value.description:
You can read more about the American Cocker Spaniel on our dedicated Cocker Spaniel page.

The American Cocker Spaniel was developed in the United States in the 19th century, originating from English Cocker Spaniels brought to America. It was bred to be smaller and have a more refined appearance. They are the smallest sporting breed recognized by the AKC. Traditionally, they were used as hunting dogs for flushing game, particularly birds. American Cocker Spaniels are known for their friendly, gentle, and playful temperament, making them excellent family pets. They are good with children and adapt well to various living situations. The breed is not rare and is popular as both a companion and show dog. Standard coat colors include black, buff, chocolate, and parti-colors (two or more colors). Unlike the English Cocker Spaniel, the American version has a shorter muzzle, rounder eyes, and a more luxurious, silky coat. English Cockers are slightly larger, have a more working-dog appearance, and are often used for hunting. Both breeds are affectionate and intelligent but differ in physical traits and energy levels.

value.description: The American Eskimo Dog, also known as the “Eskie,” is a beautiful and versatile breed that has captured the hearts of many dog lovers. Contrary to its name, this breed actually originated in Germany, not the United States. Its history can be traced back to the white German Spitz dogs that were brought to America by German immigrants in the 19th century. These dogs were bred selectively to develop the American Eskimo Dog we know today. Traditionally, American Eskimo Dogs were utilized as working dogs, excelling in various tasks such as herding, watchdog duties, and even circus performances. American Eskimo Dog is known for its stunning white coat, which is is true to breed standard, although some cream is permissible. They are not considered a highly rare breed. It is relatively popular among dog enthusiasts, especially in the United States.

value.description: The American Foxhound is a medium/large-sized breed of scent hound that originated in the United States. They were bred for hunting foxes and other small game, and are known for their strong sense of smell, endurance, and speed. They have a distinct and elegant appearance, with a lean build and a short, smooth coat which, according to the breed standard, can come in any color. American Foxhounds are energetic and require plenty of exercise to keep them physically and mentally healthy. They are independent and can be stubborn, but they are also affectionate and loyal to their owners. They are known to be good with children, but they may not be the best fit for apartment living, as they need plenty of space to run and play. They are also known for their loud baying, and can be quite vocal. They are a relatively rare breed.

value.description: The American Hairless Terrier is a small dog breed originating from the United States, specifically from Louisiana. Its history can be traced back to a single hairless puppy born in 1972 as a result of a natural mutation in a litter of Rat Terriers. This unique puppy was named Josephine, and her hairless trait became the foundation for breeding the American Hairless Terrier we know today. Traditionally, these terriers were used as versatile farm dogs, excelling in various tasks such as hunting small game, vermin control, and serving as loyal companions. They are known for their high energy, intelligence, and strong prey drive. Despite their small size, they possess a fearless and determined temperament. This hairless breed is relatively uncommon. Any and all colors and combinations are permitted except for merle and albino.

Read our dedicated page on the Amstaff and Staffy Here.


The American Staffordshire Terrier, often called AmStaff, is a strong and loyal dog breed. It originated in the United States in the 19th century, developed from the Bulldog and Terrier breeds. AmStaffs were initially used for farm work, hunting, and as family guardians. They are known for their courage, intelligence, and affectionate nature. With proper training, they make excellent family pets and are good with children. Unlike the Staffordshire Bull Terrier (Staffy), the AmStaff is larger and more muscular, with a broader head. The breed standard coat colors include solid, parti, or patched patterns in various shades, but excessive white or liver colors are discouraged. The AmStaff is recognized by the American Kennel Club (AKC) and is not considered a rare breed. An interesting fact is that despite their tough appearance, they are often referred to as "nanny dogs" due to their gentle nature with families.

They are a medium-sized breed with a dense, curly coat that comes in a variety of colors, including brown, liver, and dark chocolate. They have a strong, athletic build and a distinctive, square-shaped head. They were developed specifically for hunting in the marshes, swamps and along the shores of the Great Lakes region, making them well-suited for retrieving and hunting tasks. They have a great sense of smell and endurance, making them well-suited for hunting and retrieving in all types of water, including cold and icy water. They are considered as a rare breed.

value.description: The Anglo-French Hound is a breed of hunting dog that originated in the France, and is a crossbreed between the English Foxhound and French hound breeds. They are known for their strong hunting instincts, endurance and ability to work in tough terrain. They are a medium-sized breed with a short, dense coat that comes in tricolor (black, white and tan), bicolor (white with tan) or white with black and tan markings. They have a distinctive, square-shaped head and a muscular body. They are an active, athletic and lively breed that requires regular exercise and mental stimulation. They have a great sense of smell and endurance, making them well-suited for hunting and trailing tasks. They are considered as a rare breed, and not well-known internationally.

They are also known for their intelligence and trainability, making them well-suited for a variety of tasks such as obedience and agility competitions. They are known to be loyal and affectionate companions when given proper training and socialization. They are considered as a rare breed.

They are a medium-sized breed with a short, dense coat that comes in a pale orange fawn or sometimes brown and can be flecked with fawn or brown ticked white marks. They may also be white with brown or fawn ticks. They have a great sense of smell and endurance, making them well-suited for hunting, pointing and retrieving tasks. They have a natural tendency to point game and have a great nose, making them valuable in hunting game birds. They are considered as a rare breed, and not well-known internationally.

value.description:
Read our dedicated ACD page here.

The Australian Cattle Dog, also known as the Blue Heeler or Red Heeler, was developed in Australia in the 19th century to herd cattle over long distances in tough conditions. Traditionally, this breed was used for herding and driving cattle, showcasing its intelligence, agility, and endurance. Australian Cattle Dogs are known for their energetic, loyal, and hardworking temperament, but they can be strong-willed and require mental stimulation. They make good family pets for active households but need experienced owners to manage their high energy. The breed is not rare and is popular among farmers and dog sports enthusiasts. Standard coat colors include blue (with or without black or tan markings) and red speckle. Interestingly, they are born white (except for solid color face markings) and develop their color as they age. They are also known for their longevity, often living 12–16 years.

value.description:
Read more facts about the Australian Kelpie on their dedicated page here.

The Australian Kelpie was developed in Australia in the late 19th century to work as a herding dog, particularly for sheep. It is believed to have descended from British Collies and other working dogs. Traditionally, Kelpies are used for herding livestock in both open fields and confined spaces, showcasing their intelligence, agility, and stamina. They have a highly energetic, intelligent, and independent temperament, making them excellent working dogs but requiring experienced owners. They can be good family pets for active households but need plenty of exercise and mental stimulation. The breed is not rare in Australia but is less common elsewhere. Standard coat colors include black, red, chocolate, fawn, smoke blue, and tan, often with markings. Interestingly, Kelpies are known for their ability to work long hours in harsh conditions and are often used in dog sports like agility. They are also highly trainable and excel in obedience.

value.description:
Read more about the Aussie on our dedicated Australian Shepherd page here.

The Australian Shepherd, or "Aussie," is a highly energetic and intelligent herding dog. Despite its name, the breed was developed in the United States in the 19th century, likely from European herding dogs. Aussies are known for their agility, loyalty, and strong work ethic. They are often used as ranch dogs, in dog sports, and as service animals. Their temperament is friendly, playful, and protective, making them great family pets for active households. Australian Shepherds are very popular in the U.S. An interesting fact is that many Aussies are born with naturally bobbed tails. Breed standard coat colors include black, blue merle, red, and red merle, often with white or tan markings. Their striking eyes can be blue, brown, amber, or even heterochromatic (two different colors).

The Bracco Italiano, also known as the Italian Pointer, is a medium/large sized breed originating from Italy. Its history can be traced back to the Renaissance era, where it was used for hunting a variety of game, including birds, boars, and deers. Despite its hunting history, the Bracco Italiano is a friendly, affectionate, and intelligent breed with a strong sense of independence. Basenjis were historically used by African tribes for hunting and tracking due to their sharp sight and scent skills. They are independent and curious, with a playful and affectionate temperament. An interesting fact is they are often compared to cats because of their grooming habits and aloof nature. Breed standard coat colors include red, black, brindle, and tricolor, all with white markings. They are also known for their tightly curled tails and expressive, wrinkled foreheads.

Basset Hounds are known for their calm, friendly, and easygoing temperament, making them great family pets. They are popular for their unique appearance and gentle nature. An interesting fact is that their long ears help trap scents, aiding their tracking ability. Breed standard coat colors include tri-color (black, white, and tan), red and white, and lemon and white. Solid colors or coat patterns not matching these standards are not permitted. Their number one health concern is obesity, which can worsen joint issues like hip dysplasia and spinal problems. Regular exercise and a balanced diet are essential for their well-being.

The Bloodhound is a large scent hound breed with origins dating back to medieval Europe, developed in Belgium and France. Known for their incredible sense of smell, they were traditionally used for tracking game and people, making them valuable in search-and-rescue operations. Bloodhounds are gentle, patient, and affectionate, making them good family pets, though they can be stubborn and require consistent training. They are not a rare breed but are less common as household pets. An interesting fact is that their scent-tracking ability is legally admissible in court. Breed standard coat colors include black and tan, liver and tan, and red. Excessive white markings are not permitted. Their number one health concern is bloat (gastric torsion), a life-threatening condition. Feeding smaller meals and avoiding vigorous activity after eating can help reduce the risk.

Interestingly, they have a loose, flexible skin to protect them from bites while hunting. Standard coat colors include blue and tan, grizzle and tan, red, or wheaten. Solid black or white coats are not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are generally a healthy breed. Border Terriers are small but sturdy and require regular exercise. They are intelligent and enjoy activities like digging and chasing. Their wiry coat is low-shedding but needs regular grooming.

value.description: The Borzoi, also known as the Russian Wolfhound, is a medium/large sized breed originating from Russia. Its history can be traced back to the 17th century, where it was used for hunting wolves and other large game. Despite its hunting history, the Borzoi is a gentle, sensitive, and affectionate breed with a strong prey drive. Its long, silky coat comes in all combinations and colors except any shade of brown, blue or Isabella. and is easily recognized by its distinctive appearance. The Borzoi is still considered to be a relatively rare breed outside of its native country, although it has gained popularity in recent years. With its elegant appearance and gentle nature, the Borzoi is a beloved breed for many dog owners, especially those who appreciate its unique history and distinctive appearance. Read more about the Boston Terrier on our dedicated Boston Terrier page.

The Boston Terrier, also known as the "American Gentleman," was developed in the United States in the late 1800s. It was created by crossing Bulldogs with now-extinct white English Terriers. Originally bred for pit fighting (they originally weighed around 40 pounds) it later became a companion dog. Boston Terriers are known for their friendly, intelligent, and lively temperament. They make excellent family pets due to their affectionate and gentle nature. Interestingly, Boston Terriers were one of the first breeds developed in the U.S. Standard coat colors include black, brindle, or seal with white markings. Solid black, brindle, or seal without white is not permitted. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short snouts, which can cause breathing issues. They are also prone to eye problems and patellar luxation. Boston Terriers are small, easy to train, and adapt well to apartment living.

value.description: The Bouvier des Ardennes is a unique and versatile breed of dog with a rich history and a wide range of uses. Known for its rugged appearance and working capabilities, this breed has a number of distinctive characteristics that set it apart. The Bouvier des Ardennes is medium-sized, strong, and well-muscled, with a dense double coat that provides protection in harsh weather conditions. According to FCI breed standard, all coat colors, except solid white, are acceptable. This breed is intelligent, loyal, and protective, making it an excellent choice for guarding livestock or serving as a reliable companion. Originating from the Belgian Ardennes region, the Bouvier des Ardennes has a long history as a working dog in agriculture and herding. Today, they are also used in various dog sports such as obedience trials and agility competitions. With their unique combination of strength, intelligence, and versatility, the Bouvier des Ardennes continues to be valued for its many talents and abilities.

The Bouvier des Flandres is a medium/large sized breed originating from France. Its history can be traced back to the 19th century, where it was used for a variety of tasks such as herding cattle, guarding farms, and pulling carts. Despite its versatile history, the Bouvier des Flandres is a loyal, protective, and affectionate breed with a strong sense of independence. Its distinctive appearance includes a rough, shaggy coat that comes in standard colors of grey, brindle or black overlay. Although the Bouvier des Flandres has been a popular breed in France for many years, it is still considered to be a relatively rare breed outside of its native country. Throughout its history, the Bouvier des Flandres has been valued for its intelligence, strength, and ability to form close bonds with its owners. Today, it remains a beloved breed for many dog owners around the world.

The Boxer was developed in Germany in the late 19th century from the now-extinct Bullenbeisser and Bulldogs. They were originally used for hunting, guarding, and as working dogs. Boxers are known for their playful, energetic, and loyal temperament. They make excellent family pets due to their affectionate and protective nature. The breed is quite popular worldwide. Boxers are often called "clownish" because of their playful behavior. Standard coat colors include fawn and brindle, often with white markings. Pure white Boxers are not permitted in the breed standard due to health risks. Their number one health concern is cardiomyopathy, a heart condition. Boxers are also prone to hip dysplasia and certain cancers. They are a high-energy dog and require regular exercise and mental stimulation.

The Brussels Griffon, also known as the Petit Brabançon, is a small dog breed that originated in Belgium in the 19th century. Developed to hunt rats in stables, they later became popular as companion dogs for nobility. Brussels Griffons are known for their lively, affectionate, and curious temperament, making them great family pets for those who can meet their social needs. They are considered a rare breed. Their expressive faces often earn them the nickname "monkey face." Breed standard coat colors include red, black, black and tan, and belge (a mix of black and reddish-brown). Solid white or liver colors are not permitted. Their number one health concern is respiratory issues due to their short muzzle, which can lead to breathing difficulties. Regular vet care and avoiding overheating are important for their health.

Bull Terriers are known for their playful, energetic, and sometimes stubborn temperament, making them good family pets for active households with experienced owners. They are not a rare breed but are less common than some other breeds. Their unique egg-shaped head sets them apart from other dogs. Breed standard coat colors include white (with or without markings) and colored (like brindle, black, or red). Blue or liver colors are not permitted. According to the AKC-blue eyes are a disqualification. Their number one health concern is deafness, particularly in white-coated Bull Terriers.

The Bulldog, also known as the English Bulldog, originated in England around the 13th century. It was developed for bull-baiting, a now-banned sport. After bull-baiting ended, the breed was refined to be more companionable. Bulldogs are known for their calm, courageous, and friendly temperament. They make excellent family pets due to their affectionate nature and patience with children. The breed is quite popular worldwide. Bulldogs have a distinctive wrinkled face and a pushed-in nose. Standard coat colors include brindle, white, red, fawn, and piebald. Merle and black are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short snouts, which can cause breathing difficulties. Bulldogs are also prone to hip dysplasia and skin infections. Despite their tough appearance, they are gentle and loyal companions.

The Belgian Malinois is a medium to large-sized dog breed that originated in Belgium in the late 19th century. Known for their intelligence and work ethic, they were traditionally used as herding dogs and are now widely used in police, military, and search-and-rescue roles. Belgian Malinois are highly energetic, loyal, and protective, making them excellent working dogs but requiring experienced owners. They can be good family pets in active households with proper training. They are not a rare breed but are less common as family pets due to their high drive. They are often confused with German Shepherds but are smaller and more agile. Breed standard coat colors include fawn to mahogany with black tips and a black mask. Solid black or overly washed-out colors are not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip and elbow dysplasia, which can affect their mobility.

The Border Collie was developed in the border region between England and Scotland in the 1800s. It was bred primarily for herding sheep, known for its intelligence and agility. The breed is highly energetic, focused, and hardworking. Border Collies can make good family pets but require lots of exercise and mental stimulation. Interestingly, they are often considered the most intelligent dog breed. Standard coat colors include black and white, red and white, blue, blue merle, and sable, among others. No specific colors are prohibited, but white should not dominate. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to epilepsy and eye disorders. Border Collies excel in dog sports and need an active lifestyle. They are loyal and thrive in environments where they have a job to do.

Bullmastiffs are calm and affectionate, making them good family pets, especially with proper training.  Interestingly, they were called the "Gamekeeper's Night Dog" because they worked silently at night. Standard coat colors include fawn, red, or brindle, a very small white spot is permitted on the chest. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a common issue in larger breeds. Bullmastiffs are low-energy dogs but require regular exercise to stay healthy. They are gentle with their families but can be wary of strangers.

They are hardy and adaptable dogs, able to endure the harsh conditions of the Arctic tundra, and have been used for transportation, hunting, and guarding. With their strong work ethic, loyalty, and intelligence, Canadian Eskimo Dogs make excellent sled dogs and are still used for sledding and dog sledding races in the North. Despite their decline, the Canadian Eskimo Dog remains an important part of Canadian and Inuit cultural heritage and a symbol of their way of life.

The Cavalier King Charles Spaniel is a cheerful and affectionate breed that is known for its love of people. They are highly sociable and enjoy being part of the family, making them great pets for families and individuals alike. The breed has a silky, medium length coat that comes in four standard colors: Blenheim (chestnut and white), Ruby (solid red), Black and Tan (black and mahogany), and Tri-Color (black, white, and tan). The Cavalier King Charles Spaniel is a popular breed, although it is not considered to be as common as some of the other toy breeds. They are adaptable to different living situations and are relatively low maintenance, making them great dogs for first-time owners or those who live in apartments. value.description: The Continental Bulldog, created in Switzerland in 2004 by Imelda Angehrn, is a breed of dog known for its unique characteristics. The Continental Bulldog is a medium-sized dog with a muscular build and a distinctive wrinkled face. By FCI standard, all colors except blue and brown are allowed. The dog must have a black nose. It has a friendly and gentle temperament, making it an excellent companion for families and individuals alike. This breed is known for its loyalty and protective nature, making it an ideal guard dog as well. In terms of history, the Continental Bulldog was developed as a healthier alternative to the English Bulldog, with the goal of eliminating many of the health issues associated with the latter breed. Through careful breeding and selection, Imelda Angehrn was able to create a breed that retained the iconic look of the Bulldog while improving its overall health and vitality. Today, the Continental Bulldog is popular both as a family pet and as a show dog, showcasing its unique traits in various competitions around the world.

The Cairn Terrier was developed in the Scottish Highlands in the 1500s. It was bred to hunt vermin and small game in rocky cairns. The breed is known for its spirited, curious, and independent temperament. Cairn Terriers make good family pets due to their playful and affectionate nature. The Cairn Terrier is one of Scotland's oldest terrier breeds. According to AKC breed standard, their coat may be any color except pure white. Their number one health concern is patellar luxation, a knee condition. They are also prone to allergies and hip dysplasia. Cairn Terriers are small but sturdy and require regular grooming. They are intelligent and enjoy activities like digging and exploring. Their shaggy coat is weather-resistant.

The Chesapeake Bay Retriever, also known as the “Chessie,” is a medium/large sized dog breed originating from the United States, specifically the Chesapeake Bay area. They were developed in the 19th century to help retrieve waterfowl during hunting trips and have since become popular family pets due to their friendly and affectionate temperament. Chesapeake Bay Retrievers are known for their intelligence and trainability, but can also be independent and stubborn at times. The standard coat colors for this breed are brown and sedge (a type of reddish-brown), and their coats are thick, wavy, and water-resistant, which is a result of their origins as a water dog. Although they were once a popular breed, the Chesapeake Bay Retriever’s popularity has declined over the years and they are now considered a rare breed. Despite this, they are still highly valued for their versatility and make excellent family pets, hunting dogs, and service dogs.

The Chihuahua originated in Mexico, with roots tracing back to ancient civilizations like the Aztecs. It was developed as a companion dog and is the smallest breed in the world. Chihuahuas are known for their bold, lively, and sometimes sassy temperament. They make good family pets, especially for those in smaller living spaces. Chihuahuas are believed to have been sacred to the Aztecs. Standard coat colors include a wide range, such as black, white, fawn, chocolate, and more. No specific colors are prohibited, but patterns like merle are controversial due to health risks. Their number one health concern is dental issues due to their small mouths. They are also prone to patellar luxation and hypoglycemia. Chihuahuas are loyal and form strong bonds with their owners. They require minimal exercise but enjoy attention and playtime.

value.description: The Chinook dog breed was developed in the early 20th century in New Hampshire, USA, by Arthur Treadwell Walden. It was created as a sled dog, combining strength, speed, and endurance. Traditionally, Chinooks were used for sled pulling, freight hauling, and search-and-rescue missions. They are known for their friendly, calm, and intelligent temperament, making them excellent family pets. Chinooks are good with children and other animals, and they thrive in active households. This breed is considered rare, with a small population worldwide. Interestingly, the Chinook is the official state dog of New Hampshire and was nearly extinct in the 1980s before dedicated breeders helped revive the breed. They are versatile, adaptable, and known for their loyalty.

Their number one health concern is dental issues, especially in the hairless variety. They are also prone to skin problems and patellar luxation. Chinese Cresteds are social and thrive on human companionship. They are low-shedding but require regular skincare for the hairless type.

value.description:
Read more facts and information on our dedicated Collie page.

The Rough Collie originated in Scotland and Northern England in the 1800s, primarily as a herding dog. It was bred to work with sheep and cattle. The breed is known for its gentle, intelligent, and loyal temperament. Rough Collies make excellent family pets due to their affectionate and protective nature. The Rough Collie gained fame through the "Lassie" movies and TV shows. Standard coat colors include sable and white, tri-color (black, white, and tan), blue merle, and white. Their number one health concern is Collie Eye Anomaly (CEA), a genetic eye disorder. They are also prone to hip dysplasia and dermatomyositis. The FCI (Fédération Cynologique Internationale) considers Rough and Smooth Collies two distinct breeds, while the AKC (American Kennel Club) classifies them as one breed with two coat varieties. The Rough Collie has a long, flowing double coat, while the Smooth Collie has a short, dense coat. Both share similar temperaments and traits. Rough Collies require regular grooming to maintain their coat.

value.description: The Caucasian Ovcharka, also known as the Caucasian Shepherd, is a giant sized dog breed that originates from the Caucasus Mountains in Russia. Its history dates back centuries, where it was primarily used as a livestock guardian and protector of families. The breed is known for its strength, courage, and loyalty, making it highly valued by those who live and work in the harsh, rugged terrain of its native region. The Caucasian Ovcharka has a thick, dense coat that comes in any solid color, spotted or piebald except solid black, diluted black, blue or liver. Despite its impressive size and powerful build, the breed is known for its gentle and protective nature, making it a trustworthy and loyal companion. Although the Caucasian Ovcharka is still highly valued and used as a livestock guardian and protector in its native region, it is considered to be a rare breed outside of Russia. Due to its large size, strength, and courage, the Caucasian Ovcharka is best suited for experienced dog owners who can provide adequate exercise and training.

The Chow Chow originated in China over 2,000 years ago, making it one of the oldest dog breeds. It was used for hunting, guarding, and pulling carts. The breed is known for its aloof, independent, and loyal temperament. Chow Chows can be good family pets but require early socialization and consistent training. They are not considered a rare breed but are less common than some others. Chow Chows have a unique blue-black tongue, which is rare among dogs. Standard coat colors include red, black, blue, cinnamon, and cream. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a common issue in larger breeds. They are also prone to eye problems and entropion. Chow Chows have a thick double coat that requires regular grooming. They are dignified and reserved, often forming strong bonds with their families.

The Catahoula Leopard Dog, also known as the Louisiana Catahoula, originated in the United States, specifically in Louisiana, in the 18th century. It was developed as a working dog for hunting, herding, and guarding livestock. The breed is known for its independent, energetic, and protective temperament. Catahoula's can be good family pets with proper training and socialization but are best suited for active households. They are not considered a rare breed but are less common outside the southern U.S. They are the official state dog of Louisiana. Standard coat colors include a wide range, such as blue merle, red merle, brindle, and solid colors.  Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are generally hardy. They are also prone to deafness, especially in dogs with white coats. Catahoula's are highly intelligent and require plenty of exercise and mental stimulation. Their unique coat patterns and striking eyes make them visually distinctive.

The Dachshund, also known as the "wiener dog," originated in Germany in the 16th century. It was developed to hunt badgers and other burrowing animals, thanks to its long body and short legs. The breed is known for its curious, brave, and sometimes stubborn temperament. Dachshunds make good family pets but require consistent training and socialization. Dachshunds come in two sizes (standard and miniature) and three coat types (smooth, longhaired, and wirehaired). Standard coat colors include red, black and tan, chocolate, and dappled, among others. Their number one health concern is intervertebral disc disease (IVDD), due to their long spine, which can lead to back problems. They are also prone to obesity and dental issues. Dachshunds are energetic and enjoy activities like digging and exploring.

The Dalmatian originated in Croatia, with roots tracing back to the 18th century, though its exact history is unclear. It was traditionally used as a carriage dog, guarding horses and coaches, and later as a firehouse mascot. The breed is known for its energetic, intelligent, and outgoing temperament. Dalmatians can be good family pets but require plenty of exercise and mental stimulation. Dalmatians are born completely white and develop their spots as they grow. Standard coat colors are white with black or liver (brown) spots. Tri-color or lemon (yellow) spots are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is deafness, which is common in the breed. They are also prone to urinary stones and hip dysplasia. Dalmatians are highly active and thrive in environments where they can run and play. Their unique spotted coat makes them one of the most recognizable dog breeds.

The Doberman Pinscher was developed in Germany in the late 19th century by Louis Dobermann, a tax collector who wanted a loyal guard dog. It was bred for protection, police work, and companionship. The breed is known for its loyal, intelligent, and fearless temperament. Dobermans can be good family pets with proper training and socialization but are best suited for experienced owners. Dobermans are one of the few breeds specifically created for personal protection. Standard coat colors include black, red, blue, and fawn, all with rust markings. White or albino Dobermans are not permitted in the breed standard due to health risks. Their number one health concern is dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM), a heart condition. They are also prone to hip dysplasia and von Willebrand’s disease, a bleeding disorder. Dobermans are highly energetic and require regular exercise and mental stimulation. Their sleek, muscular build and alert nature make them excellent working dogs and loyal companions.

The Dogo Argentino was developed in Argentina in the 1920s by Dr. Antonio Nores Martínez for big-game hunting and protection. It was bred from a mix of strong breeds, including the Cordoba Fighting Dog. The breed is known for its loyal, confident, and courageous temperament. Dogo Argentinos can be good family pets with proper training and socialization but are best suited for experienced owners. They are considered a rare breed outside of Argentina. The Dogo Argentino is the only Argentine breed recognized internationally. Standard coat color is solid white, with a small black spot on the head allowed. Any other colors or patterns are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is deafness, which is common in white-coated breeds. They are also prone to hip dysplasia and skin allergies. Dogo Argentinos are strong, energetic, and require regular exercise and mental stimulation. Their muscular build and protective nature make them excellent working dogs and loyal companions.

The English Cocker Spaniel originated in England in the 19th century and was developed as a hunting dog, specifically for flushing game birds. The breed is known for its cheerful, affectionate, and energetic temperament. English Cocker Spaniels make excellent family pets due to their friendly and adaptable nature. They were originally bred alongside Springer Spaniels and were only recognized as a separate breed in the 20th century.  Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to ear infections and eye disorders. Compared to the American Cocker Spaniel, the English Cocker is larger, has a longer muzzle, and a less domed head. The English Cocker is also more focused on working and hunting, while the American Cocker is often bred more for companionship. Both breeds have silky coats but require regular grooming.

Read more facts and information on our dedicated French Bulldog page.

The French Bulldog, or "Frenchie," was developed in France in the 1800s, originating from small English Bulldogs brought by lace workers. It was bred as a companion dog. The breed is known for its playful, affectionate, and easygoing temperament. French Bulldogs make excellent family pets due to their adaptable and loving nature. They are extremely popular worldwide. Their bat-like ears are a defining feature and set them apart from other bulldog breeds. Standard coat colors include brindle, fawn, white, and pied (white with patches of another color). Solid black, blue, chocolate, or merle coats are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short snouts, which can cause breathing difficulties. They are also prone to hip dysplasia, spinal issues, and heat sensitivity. French Bulldogs are low-energy dogs but enjoy playtime and companionship. Their compact size and charming personality make them ideal for apartment living.

value.description:
Read more facts and information about the German Shorthaired Pointer on their page.

The German Shorthaired Pointer (GSP) was developed in Germany in the 19th century as a versatile hunting dog. It was bred to point, retrieve, and track game on both land and water. The breed is known for its energetic, intelligent, and friendly temperament. German Shorthaired Pointers can be good family pets but require plenty of exercise and mental stimulation. GSPs are one of the most versatile hunting breeds, capable of working in various terrains and climates. Standard coat colors include liver (brown), black, or a combination of these with white, such as solid liver, liver and white, or roan. Solid white lemon, orange or tan areas are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to bloat and eye disorders. German Shorthaired Pointers are highly active and thrive in environments where they can run, swim, and explore. Their athleticism and loyalty make them excellent hunting companions and family pets.

value.description: The German Wirehaired Pointer, aka Deutsch Drahthaar, is a versatile hunting dog that originated in Germany in the late 19th century. It was bred to be an all-purpose gun dog, excelling in hunting, pointing, and retrieving both on land and in water. This breed is known for its intelligent, energetic, and loyal nature, making it a great companion for active families. They are affectionate with their owners but may be wary of strangers. With proper training and socialization, they can be excellent family dogs, though they need plenty of exercise to stay happy. Their coat is rough and wiry, providing protection in harsh conditions, and comes in standard colors like brown or black roan, brown with white and light roan, often with patches. While closely related the the German Shorthaired pointer, they are considered a different breed.

The Giant Schnauzer originated in Germany in the 17th century, developed from smaller Schnauzers and other working breeds. It was bred as a versatile farm dog for herding, guarding, and driving cattle. The breed is known for its bold, intelligent, and loyal temperament. Giant Schnauzers can be good family pets with proper training and socialization but are best suited for experienced owners. They were later used as police and military dogs due to their strength and trainability. Standard coat colors include solid black or pepper and salt (gray). No other colors are permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to eye problems and bloat. Giant Schnauzers are highly energetic and require regular exercise and mental stimulation. Their strong, protective nature and distinctive bearded appearance make them excellent working dogs and loyal companions.

The Golden Retriever was developed in Scotland in the mid-19th century by Lord Tweedmouth for retrieving game during hunting. It was bred to be a skilled and gentle working dog. The breed is known for its friendly, intelligent, and loyal temperament. Golden Retrievers make excellent family pets due to their affectionate and patient nature. They are one of the most popular breeds worldwide. Golden Retrievers are often used as service dogs, therapy dogs, and search-and-rescue dogs due to their trainability and gentle demeanor. Standard coat colors include shades of gold, from light to dark. White, red, or black coats are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to cancer and heart conditions. Golden Retrievers are highly energetic and thrive in active households. Their love for people and eagerness to please make them ideal companions for families.

value.description:
Read more facts and information on our dedicated Great Dane page.

The Great Dane is a large dog breed that originated in Germany around the 16th century. It was developed by crossing English Mastiffs with Irish Wolfhounds, initially used for hunting wild boar and guarding estates. Known as the "gentle giant," the Great Dane has a friendly, patient, and loyal temperament, making it an excellent family pet. Despite its size, it is generally good with children and other animals. The breed is not rare but is less common than smaller dogs. Great Danes come in several standard coat colors, including fawn, brindle, blue, black, harlequin (white with black patches), and mantle (black and white). Their number one health concern is bloat (gastric torsion), a life-threatening condition that requires immediate attention. Great Danes have a relatively short lifespan, typically living 7-10 years.

Read more facts and information about the Great Pyrenees on their page.

The Great Pyrenees is an ancient dog breed developed in the Pyrenees Mountains between France and Spain hundreds of years ago. Traditionally, it was used as a livestock guardian, protecting sheep from predators like wolves and bears. This breed has a calm, patient, and protective temperament, making it a good family pet, especially in homes with space for its large size. It is not a rare breed but is less common than some other dogs. An interesting fact is that the Great Pyrenees has a double dewclaw on its hind legs, a unique trait for herding dogs. The breed standard coat color is white, often with patches of gray, tan, or badger (a mix of brown and black). Solid colors other than white are not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in large breeds. Great Pyrenees are known for their independence and strong guarding instincts.

The breed is known for its large size, strong build, and beautiful tricolor coat of black, white, and rust. Greater Swiss Mountain Dogs have a friendly and affectionate temperament, making them good family pets. They are highly trainable and have a high energy level, making them suitable for various activities such as obedience, herding trials, and cart pulling. The standard coat color for the Greater Swiss Mountain Dog is tricolor (black, white, and rust). Although not considered a rare breed, the Greater Swiss Mountain Dog is not as commonly seen as other popular breeds.

value.description:
Read more facts and information on our dedicated Greyhound page.

The Greyhound is one of the oldest dog breeds, dating back over 4,000 years, and was developed in ancient Egypt and later refined in Europe. Traditionally, it was used for hunting and coursing game due to its incredible speed, and later became popular in dog racing. Greyhounds have a gentle, calm, and affectionate temperament, making them excellent family pets. They are not a rare breed but are less common as household pets. An interesting fact is that Greyhounds are the fastest dog breed, reaching speeds of almost 40 mph. The breed standard allows for a wide variety of coat colors, including black, white, fawn, blue, and brindle. No coat colors are prohibited. Their number one health concern is osteosarcoma (bone cancer), which is more common in larger breeds. Despite their athleticism, Greyhounds are often couch potatoes and enjoy lounging indoors.

The Griffon Belge, or Brussels Griffon, is a small dog breed developed in Belgium during the 1800s. It was originally bred to hunt and eliminate rats in stables. This breed has a lively, affectionate, and sometimes stubborn temperament, making it a good family pet for those who enjoy its quirky personality. The Griffon Belge is considered a rare breed, especially outside of Europe. An interesting fact is that its distinctive facial expression resembles that of a monkey or a human, earning it the nickname "monkey face." The breed standard coat colors for the Griffon Belge are black or black and tan. Other colors, like white or parti-colors, are not permitted. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short muzzle, which can cause breathing difficulties. Regular grooming is needed to maintain their wiry or smooth coat.

The German Shepherd was developed in Germany in the late 19th century by Captain Max von Stephanitz for herding and guarding sheep. It was later used for police, military, and search-and-rescue work. The breed is known for its intelligent, loyal, and confident temperament. German Shepherds make excellent family pets with proper training and socialization but require an active lifestyle. They are one of the most popular breeds worldwide. German Shepherds are often used as service dogs due to their versatility and trainability. Standard coat colors include black and tan, sable, and solid black. Blue, liver, or white coats are not permitted in the breed standard. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a common issue in larger breeds. They are also prone to degenerative myelopathy and bloat. German Shepherds are highly energetic and thrive in environments where they have a job to do. Their intelligence and loyalty make them exceptional working dogs and companions.

value.description: The FCI (Fédération Cynologique Internationale) recognizes the Irish Red and White Setter and the Irish Setter as separate breeds. While the most obvious difference is their coat color, there are several other distinctions in their build, temperament, and history. The Irish Red and White Setter is slightly shorter, compact and muscular. They retain many of their original working dog instincts and tend to be slightly easier to train. They are primarily field dogs and working gun dogs. Irish Setters have been refined to have a beautiful red coat and show friendly appearance. Irish Red and White Setters, while they make great pets, are better suited as a traditional, working and hunting dog.

value.description:
Read more facts and information on our dedicated Irish Setter Page.

The Irish Setter is a dog breed developed in Ireland during the 18th century. It was originally bred as a gundog for hunting birds, known for its excellent scenting ability and speed. This breed has a friendly, energetic, and outgoing temperament, making it a great family pet for active households.  An interesting fact is that they were initially red and white before solid red became the preferred color. The breed standard coat color is rich mahogany or chestnut red; any black in the coat is not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though they are also prone to bloat and certain genetic conditions like progressive retinal atrophy. Irish Setters require regular exercise and grooming due to their long, silky coat.

value.description:
Read more facts and information on our Irish Wolfhound page.

The Irish Wolfhound is an ancient breed developed in Ireland over 2,000 years ago. It was traditionally used for hunting wolves, elk, and other large game, as well as guarding estates. This breed has a gentle, loyal, and calm temperament, making it a good family pet despite its massive size. Irish Wolfhounds are considered a rare breed due to their low population numbers. An interesting fact is that they are one of the tallest dog breeds in the world, often standing over 30 inches at the shoulder. The breed standard allows a variety of coat colors, including gray, brindle, red, black, white, and fawn. No specific colors are prohibited, but unusual patterns like merle are not accepted. Their number one health concern is cardiomyopathy, a heart condition, and they also have a relatively short lifespan of 6-8 years. Regular exercise and space are important for this giant breed.

value.description:
Read more facts and information about the JRT /Parson Russell Terrier on their page.

The Jack Russell Terrier (JRT) was developed in England in the 1800s by Reverend John Russell for fox hunting and earthwork. It was bred to be small, agile, and fearless. The breed is known for its energetic, intelligent, and bold temperament. Jack Russell Terriers can be good family pets but require an active lifestyle and consistent training. They are often confused with the Parson Russell Terrier, but the JRT is smaller and more rectangular, while the Parson Russell is taller and more square-shaped. Standard coat colors include white with black, tan, or brown markings. Their number one health concern is patellar luxation, a knee condition. They are also prone to deafness and eye disorders. Both breeds share a common history and high energy levels, but the JRT is more compact and excels at earthwork, while the Parson Russell is taller and better suited for structured activities. The FCI recognizes both breeds, while the AKC only recognizes the Parson Russell Terrier. The UKC and JRTCA recognize the Jack Russell Terrier.

value.description:
Read more facts about the Newfoundland dog on their page.

The Landseer is a large dog breed that originated in Canada. It is a variation of the Newfoundland dog, and is named after the English painter Sir Edwin Henry Landseer who often featured the breed in his works. The Newfoundland Dog and the Landseer (European Continental Type) are considered two distinct breeds by the Fédération Cynologique Internationale (FCI), whereas other kennel clubs, such as the American Kennel Club (AKC) and The Kennel Club (UK), classify the Landseer as just a color variation of the Newfoundland. The breed was traditionally used for rescue work in the icy waters of Newfoundland, as well as for hauling heavy loads and working as a draft animal. Despite their large size and strength, the Landseer is known for its gentle and patient temperament, making it an ideal family companion. The standard coat colors for the breed are black and white, with a distinctive white blaze on the chest. The Landseer is considered to be a relatively rare breed, and is not as commonly found as other popular breeds.

The Landseer is considered more athletic and lively then the Newfoundland. It's head tends to be more refined and elongated and it's coat it a bit shorter and coarser in texture.

The breed standard recognizes black and tan as the traditional coat color, although some also have a black and tan with a mahogany cast. The Manchester Terrier is considered a rare breed, with only a limited number of breeding dogs worldwide. Manchester Terriers come in two distinct sizes: Standard and Toy. The Standard Manchester Terrier typically stands between 15 and 16 inches tall at the shoulder and weighs around 12 to 22 pounds. They are the larger of the two sizes and possess a sleek and muscular build. On the other hand, the Toy Manchester Terrier stands between 10 and 12 inches tall and weighs around 6 to 8 pounds. Despite their smaller size, Toy Manchester Terriers are equally elegant and agile. Both sizes of Manchester Terriers are known for their keen intelligence, alertness, and affectionate nature.

value.description:
More facts and information on our dedicated Newfoundland page.

The Newfoundland dog breed originated in Canada, specifically Newfoundland, in the 18th century. They were traditionally used as working dogs for fishermen, helping to pull nets, haul carts, and rescue people from water. Newfoundlands are known for their gentle, calm, and patient temperament. They make excellent family pets, especially for homes with children, due to their protective and affectionate nature. Newfoundlands have webbed feet, making them powerful swimmers. Breed standard coat colors include black, brown, gray, and Landseer (white with black markings). No coat colors are officially prohibited, but mismarked or brindle coats are discouraged. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in large breeds. Regular exercise and weight management are important for their overall health.

Today, Otterhounds are friendly, good-natured and boisterous dogs, known for their strong vocalization and energetic temperament. They are known to be great with children and other pets. The standard coat colors for an Otterhound are black, blue, and tan, (all hound colors) with rough, dense fur that requires regular grooming. They are a relatively rare breed, with a small gene pool, and are recognized by the American Kennel Club.

The Papillon, also known as the Continental Toy Spaniel, is a small-sized dog breed that originated in France. The breed has a long history as a companion dog, dating back several centuries, and was popular among royalty and the aristocracy. The Papillon is known for its lively and playful temperament, making them great family pets. They are intelligent and eager to please, making them easy to train, but also require plenty of mental stimulation to stay happy and healthy. The standard base coat color for Papillons is white with patches in any color. The breed is also characterized by its distinctive “butterfly” ears, which are large and fringed with hair. The breed is becoming increasingly popular due to its cute and charming appearance, as well as its affectionate nature and trainability.

Phalènes and Papillons are often born in the same litter but are considered separate breeds by the FCI. The Pomeranian originated from the region of Pomerania, which spans across modern-day Germany and Poland. Descending from larger sled-pulling and herding dogs, Pomeranians were selectively bred to be smaller in size, eventually becoming a favorite companion of European royalty and aristocracy. Traditionally, Pomeranians were utilized as working dogs, performing tasks such as herding livestock and alerting their owners to potential dangers. Over time, their compact size and charming demeanor led to their popularity as companion animals. Despite their diminutive stature, Pomeranians possess a confident and lively temperament. They are known for their intelligence, curiosity, and affectionate nature. According to breed standard, all coat colors, variations and patterns are acceptable.

The Standard Poodle, the largest size, stands over 15 inches tall and typically weighs between 45 and 70 pounds. The Miniature Poodle, standing between 10 and 15 inches tall, weighs between 15 and 18 pounds, making them a versatile companion that combines intelligence and charm. They are well-suited for apartment living and are popular in various dog sports. Finally, the Toy Poodle, measuring under 10 inches tall, weighs around 6 to 9 pounds. They are delightful and compact companions that thrive in smaller living spaces. All three sizes share the breed’s trademark curly or corded coat, intelligence, and trainability, making them well-rounded and loyal companions for dog lovers.

value.description:
Read more about the Parson Russell Terrier/JRT on their page.

The Parson Russell Terrier was developed in England in the 1800s by Reverend John Russell for fox hunting. It was bred to be a small, agile, and fearless working dog. The breed is known for its energetic, intelligent, and bold temperament. Parson Russell Terriers can be good family pets but require an active lifestyle and consistent training. They are often confused with the Jack Russell Terrier (JRT), but the Parson Russell has longer legs and a more square-shaped body, while the JRT is smaller and more rectangular. Standard coat colors include white with black, tan, or brown markings. Their number one health concern is patellar luxation, a knee condition. They are also prone to deafness and eye disorders. Both breeds share a common history and high energy levels, but the Parson Russell is taller and more suited to structured activities, while the JRT is more compact and often used for earthwork. Both require plenty of exercise and mental stimulation. The AKC does not recognize the Jack Russell terrier while the FCI does.

value.description:
Read more facts and information about the Pekingese on their page.

The Pekingese originated in ancient China over 2,000 years ago, where it was bred as a companion dog for Chinese royalty. It was highly revered and often kept in imperial palaces. The breed is known for its dignified, loyal, and sometimes stubborn temperament. Pekingese can be good family pets but are best suited for quieter households. They are not considered a rare breed but are less common than some others. Pekingese were believed to be sacred and were sometimes called "lion dogs" due to their resemblance to Chinese guardian lions. Standard coat colors include a wide range, such as black, white, fawn, red, and cream. No specific colors are prohibited, but albino or liver-colored coats are less desirable. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short snouts, which can cause breathing difficulties. They are also prone to eye problems and joint issues like patellar luxation. Pekingese have a long, flowing coat that requires regular grooming. They are small, low-energy dogs but enjoy short walks and attention from their owners.

value.description:
Read more facts and information about the Petit Brabancon on the Brussels Griffon page.

One of the 3 verities of Brussels Griffon.

  1. Bruxellois (Brussels Griffon): Has a rough, wiry coat and is typically red in color.

  2. Griffon Belge (Belgian Griffon): Also has a rough, wiry coat but comes in black, black and tan, or other approved colors (not red).

  3. Petit Brabançon: Has a smooth, short coat and can be any of the accepted colors, including red, black, or black and tan.

While they share similar temperaments and origins, their coat types and colors distinguish them as varieties within the same breed standard.

The Pug originated in China over 2,000 years ago and was later developed in Europe, particularly in the Netherlands, where it became a favorite of royalty. It was bred as a companion dog. Their number one health concern is brachycephalic syndrome, due to their short snouts, which can cause breathing difficulties. They are also prone to obesity, eye problems, and hip dysplasia. Pugs have a short, smooth coat that is easy to maintain but sheds heavily. They are small, low-energy dogs but enjoy playtime and attention from their families.

The Rhodesian Ridgeback originated in southern Africa in the 19th century, developed by Boer farmers. They were traditionally used for hunting lions and guarding property. Rhodesian Ridgebacks are known for their loyal, intelligent, and independent temperament. They can make good family pets but require consistent training and socialization due to their strong-willed nature. The breed is not extremely rare but is less common than some other breeds. They have a distinctive "ridge" of hair along the back, which grows in the opposite direction of the rest of their coat. Breed standard coat colors range from light wheaten to red wheaten; black, brindle, or white coats are not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in larger breeds. Regular exercise and mental stimulation are important for their well-being.

The Rottweiler is an ancient breed originating in Germany, dating back to Roman times. They were traditionally used as herding dogs, guard dogs, and for pulling carts. Rottweilers are known for their confident, loyal, and protective temperament. They can make excellent family pets with proper training and socialization, but their strong nature requires an experienced owner. Rottweilers were once used to carry money in pouches around their necks, earning them the nickname "butcher's dogs." Breed standard coat colors are black with clearly defined tan or mahogany markings; no other colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in larger breeds. Regular exercise and a balanced diet are essential for their overall health.

They were traditionally used for herding reindeer, pulling sleds, and keeping their owners warm in cold climates. Samoyeds are known for their friendly, gentle, and social temperament. They make excellent family pets and are great with children due to their playful and affectionate nature. Samoyeds have a "smiling" expression due to the shape of their eyes and mouth. Breed standard coat colors are white, cream, or biscuit; pure white is most common, and no other colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in larger breeds. Regular grooming is essential to maintain their thick, double coat.

The Standard Schnauzer originated in Germany in the 15th century, making it the oldest of the three Schnauzer breeds. They were traditionally used as versatile farm dogs for herding, guarding, and ratting. Standard Schnauzers are known for their intelligent, spirited, and loyal temperament. They make good family pets but require consistent training and mental stimulation.  An interesting fact is that Standard Schnauzers were used as messenger dogs during World War I. Breed standard coat colors include salt-and-pepper and pure black; no other colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in medium to large breeds. Compared to the Miniature and Giant Schnauzers, the Standard Schnauzer is more balanced in temperament—less feisty than the Miniature and less intense than the Giant, making them a versatile and adaptable companion.

Scotties are one of the oldest Highland breeds and were favored by several U.S. presidents. Breed standard coat colors include black, wheaten, and brindle; no other colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is Scottie cramp, a muscle disorder, and they are also prone to von Willebrand's disease, a bleeding disorder.

The Shar Pei originated in China over 2,000 years ago, where they were developed as versatile farm dogs. They were traditionally used for hunting, herding, and guarding. Shar Peis are known for their loyal, independent, and sometimes aloof temperament. They can make good family pets but require early socialization and consistent training. The breed was once rare and nearly extinct but has regained popularity. An interesting fact is that their loose, wrinkled skin was originally meant to help them escape predators by making it harder to grab hold of them. Breed standard coat colors include solid shades like black, cream, fawn, and sable; no multicolored or patterned coats are permitted. Their number one health concern is Shar Pei fever, a condition that can lead to kidney or liver issues. Regular care is needed to manage their skin folds and prevent infections.

value.description: The Sloughi, also known as the Arabian Greyhound, is a medium sized breed that originated in North Africa. It was originally used for hunting, especially for larger game such as gazelle and hare. The Sloughi has a sleek, slender build and is known for its speed and endurance. The standard coat color for the Sloughi is from light fawn to reddish-brown, with or without bridling and black marks. They are a rare breed outside of Africa, but have a dedicated following in their country of origin and among those who appreciate the unique traits of this ancient breed. The Sloughi is an intelligent and affectionate breed, known for its strong bond with its family and its calm and independent temperament. They are not well suited to families with young children, as they are sensitive and may be easily frightened by rough play. The Sloughi requires a lot of exercise and mental stimulation to stay happy and healthy, and they make excellent companions for active households and those who appreciate their elegance and grace. Despite their rarity, the Sloughi is a highly valued breed for its unique beauty and its loyal and loving nature.

Spinoni make excellent family pets and are great with children due to their gentle and sociable nature. The breed is relatively rare outside of Italy but is gaining popularity. The Spinone is one of the oldest gun dog breeds, known for its distinctive wiry coat and "Roman nose." Breed standard coat colors include white, orange, brown, and roan patterns; no solid black or tricolor coats are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in larger breeds. Regular exercise and grooming are important for their well-being.

The Saint Bernard originated in the Swiss Alps in the 17th century, developed by monks at the Saint Bernard Hospice. They were traditionally used for rescue work in the mountains, saving travelers from avalanches and harsh weather. Saint Bernards are known for their gentle, friendly, and patient temperament. They make excellent family pets and are great with children due to their calm and protective nature. Saint Bernards were originally smaller and had shorter coats, but they were crossed with larger breeds to enhance their rescue abilities. Breed standard coat colors include white with red, mahogany, or brindle markings; solid colors or lack of white markings are not permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition common in large breeds. Regular exercise and weight management are important for their overall health.

The Staffordshire Bull Terrier, or Staffie, originated in England in the 19th century, developed for bull-baiting and later as a companion dog. They are known for their courageous, affectionate, and loyal temperament. Staffies make excellent family pets and are great with children, earning them the nickname "nanny dog." The breed is popular in many countries. An interesting fact is that Staffies were bred to be both strong and gentle, combining tenacity with a loving nature. Breed standard coat colors include black, blue, brindle, red, fawn, and white, often with white markings; no black-and-tan or liver colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition, though they are also prone to skin allergies.

The Wire-haired variety was developed in the 20th century by crossing the Short-haired Vizsla with other wire-haired breeds, like the German Wirehaired Pointer, to create a dog better suited for harsh terrain and colder climates. While both share similar temperaments and hunting abilities, the Wire-haired Vizsla has a denser, coarser coat and a more robust build compared to the sleek, short-haired version. The AKC (American Kennel Club) considers the Wire-haired Vizsla and the Short-haired Vizsla as part of the same breed, simply as coat varieties of the Vizsla. In the AKC, both are registered under the same breed standard, with the primary difference being their coat type. This differs from the FCI, which classifies them as two distinct breeds. In the AKC, both share the same temperament, hunting abilities, and overall characteristics, with the Wire-haired variety having a denser, wiry coat suited for rougher conditions.

Breed standard coat colors are various shades of golden rust; no other colors are permitted. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, a joint condition, though they are also prone to epilepsy and allergies. Wire-haired and short-haired Vizslas are considered the same breed by the AKC BUT separate breeds by the FCI. The wire-haired variety has a denser, coarser coat and is slightly larger, with a more rugged appearance suited for harsh terrain.

value.description: The Wire-haired Pointing Griffon is a medium sized dog breed that originated in France. It was developed as a hunting dog, specifically for pointing and retrieving game such as birds and rabbits. The breed is known for its excellent scenting abilities, friendly nature, and strong work ethic, making it a popular choice for hunters and families alike. The standard coat colors for the Wire-haired Pointing Griffon are brown and silver in several variations. Despite its popularity in France, the breed is relatively rare outside of its native country. The Wire-haired Pointing Griffon is a friendly and outgoing breed that thrives on human companionship.

value.description: The Yakutian Laika is a medium sized dog breed that originated in Russia. It was developed as a sled dog and hunting dog, used for pulling sleds in harsh, snowy environments and for hunting game such as foxes and hares. The breed is known for its strong work ethic, high energy, and independent nature, making it a popular choice for those who live in snowy areas or enjoy dog sledding. The standard coat color for the Yakutian Laika is white with any color patching, either bicolor or tricolor. Despite its popularity in Russia, the breed is relatively rare outside of its native country. The Yakutian Laika is an independent breed that requires a lot of exercise and mental stimulation, making it best suited for experienced dog owners who have the time and energy to provide for its needs

value.description:
More facts and information on the Weimaraner page.

The Weimaraner originated in Germany in the early 19th century, developed as a versatile hunting dog for large game like boar and deer. They are known for their intelligent, energetic, and loyal temperament. Weimaraners can make good family pets but require lots of exercise and mental stimulation due to their high energy levels. Weimaraners are often called the "Gray Ghost" due to their unique silver-gray coat. Breed standard coat colors include shades of gray, from mouse-gray to silver-gray; blue and black coats are not permitted. Their number one health concern is bloat (gastric torsion), a life-threatening condition common in deep-chested breeds. Long-haired Weimaraners are recognized by some kennel clubs, like the FCI, but they do not meet the AKC breed standard, which only allows short coats.

value.description:
More facts and information on the Corgi page.

The Corgi breed includes two distinct types: the Welsh Corgi (Cardigan) and the Welsh Corgi (Pembroke), both originating in Wales. The Cardigan is the older breed, dating back over 3,000 years, while the Pembroke was developed later, around the 10th century. Both were traditionally used as herding dogs for cattle. Corgis are known for their intelligent, alert, and affectionate temperament. They make excellent family pets and are great with children, though their herding instincts may lead to nipping. Neither breed is rare, with Pembrokes being more popular due to their association with the British royal family. Pembrokes have no tail (or a very short one), while Cardigans have long tails. Breed standard coat colors for Pembrokes include red, sable, fawn, and black-and-tan, with or without white markings; blue or brindle coats are not permitted. Cardigans come in more colors, including brindle and blue merle, but white-dominated coats are not allowed. Their number one health concern is hip dysplasia, though Pembrokes are also prone to degenerative myelopathy. The main differences between the two are their tails, coat colors, and body structure—Cardigans are slightly larger and have rounded ears, while Pembrokes are more compact with pointed ears.

value.description: The Welsh Springer Spaniel is a medium sized dog breed that originated in Wales. The breed is known for its excellent nose, high energy, and friendly nature, making it a popular choice for hunters and families alike. The standard coat colors for the Welsh Springer Spaniel are red and white. Despite its popularity, the breed is relatively rare and can be difficult to find outside of its native country. The Welsh Springer Spaniel is an affectionate and outgoing breed that thrives on human companionship, making it an ideal choice for those who are looking for a loyal and loving companion.
